title: micromeria fruticosa subsp. serpyllifolia
intro: Micromeria fruticosa subsp. serpyllifolia, also known as Creeping Thyme or Greek Mountain Tea, is a subspecies of the Micromeria fruticosa plant, a low-growing, creeping evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This herb has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Micromeria fruticosa subsp. serpyllifolia include its ability to boost the immune system, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
